Positioning Meter

As of 2026-05-08 · 346 names with composite temperature · V1.4 + min-buckets · Backtest IC −0.022 (3m fwd)

📘 Glossary — what every number means

Temperature (0–100)

The composite "how hot/late" score. Each name is ranked vs its own trailing 5y history (0 = coldest ever, 100 = hottest ever) AND vs cluster peers, then those two are blended. The composite averages bucket scores. High temperature = positioning + momentum + valuation are all stretched ⇒ historically associated with negative forward returns at extremes (V1.4 backtest IC −0.022 at 3m).

Bucket scores (Pos / Val / Tech)

Each is 0–100, average of the underlying signals (each signal scored as percentile vs own history & cluster peers). Click any ticker to see the signal-by-signal breakdown.

  • Pos — Positioning (insider Form 4, short volume, true SI)
  • Val — Valuation (TTM P/E, EV/Sales)
  • Tech — Technical/momentum (returns, RSI, distance from 200d MA, % from 52w high)

Conviction (0–100)

How much the buckets agree. High conviction = all buckets pointing the same way (all hot, or all cold). Low conviction = buckets disagree (e.g., expensive valuation but cold technicals — value-trap risk). 100 = perfect agreement; 0 = wide spread.

Anomaly count

Number of individual signals where this ticker is at the 90th+ percentile vs its cluster peers today. High = name stands out from peers across many measures. Low = blends in.

7d Δ (temperature change)

Today's temperature minus temperature 5 trading days ago. Catches names heating up or cooling off recently. Positive (red) = heating up. Negative (green, contrarian-favorable) = cooling.

Compound flags

  • Late — Pos ≥ 85, Val ≥ 80, Tech ≥ 85. Triple-extreme stretched.
  • Wash — Pos ≤ 15, Val ≤ 25, Tech ≤ 15. Triple-extreme washed-out.
  • Earnings — earnings within next 14 days.

Why colors are inverted

This tool is contrarian-oriented: high temperature = "overheated, expect mean reversion" = red/danger. Low temperature = "washed out, expect bounce" = green/opportunity. Opposite of typical price-momentum coloring.

Backtest validation

V1.4 composite IC −0.022 at 3-month forward horizon (Spearman). Bottom decile → 56% positive forward return; top decile → 56% negative forward return. Strongest individual signal: si_true_dtc (NASDAQ days-to-cover) IC −0.064 at 3m. See backtest_report.md for full per-signal metrics.

Methodology (click to expand)

Universe: 366 TMT names (mcap ≥ $1.5B) from theme_detector.

Signals: 18 total computed daily — 13 in composite, 5 overlay-only. See QUESTIONS.md for backtest-driven inclusion decisions (e.g. trend signals like 12m return excluded because positive IC means they're not contrarian).

Dual percentile: each signal is scored vs (a) own 5y rolling history and (b) cluster peers cross-section. Bucket scores average those.

Composite: weighted average of bucket scores. Initial weights pos 0.25 / val 0.20 / tech 0.20 (flows + options not yet implemented). Reweighted when buckets are missing for a name. Min 2 buckets required.

Backtest: 10y daily panel, IC = Spearman correlation between signal percentile and forward return. V1.4 composite IC −0.022 at 3m fwd, decile spread −2.30%, bot decile hit 56%. See data/backtest_report.md.

Limitations: options bucket not implemented (would require Polygon $200/mo). ETF flows forward-only. EPS revision overlay shows current snapshot only. 13F has 45-day reporting lag and is long-only. NASDAQ true SI covers only NASDAQ-listed names (~65% of universe).
